reasoning: Q6 has a critical semantic bug: it parses CSV text files instead of using binary columnar storage, causing 627x slowdown vs DuckDB (12.5s vs 20ms). The fix is mandatory and low-risk. Once binary storage is implemented via mmap-based .col file reading, execution should reach competitive performance (~20-30ms), enabling effective performance tuning in subsequent iterations.
focus_areas: Switch from CSV text parsing to mmap-based binary columnar storage
Load .col files for l_shipdate, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_quantity columns
Use gendb_dir parameter to locate binary files in tpch_sf10.gendb/
Eliminate std::getline and strtok-like character parsing
strategy_notes: This iteration focuses exclusively on the critical storage fix. Do NOT attempt performance optimizations (zone maps, SIMD, predicate reordering) until binary storage is working correctly. The semantic bug is blocking all meaningful optimization. After this fix, expect execution time to drop from 12.5s to ~20-30ms. The next iteration can then add zone map skipping (5-10x improvement) and predicate reordering (3-5% improvement). Current parallelism and aggregation design are already sound for 64 cores.
notes: Correctness first: binary storage fix addresses the root cause (semantic bug), not a symptom. Once implemented, profile the code to identify which of the performance optimizations (zone maps, predicate reordering, SIMD) yield the best returns with remaining 2 iterations. Priority order for next iteration: (1) Zone Map Skipping (5-10x), (2) Predicate Reordering (3-5%), (3) SIMD filtering (2-3x) — but only if profiling identifies CPU as bottleneck after storage is fixed.
